checks.a_analysis: The output contains only information present in the source document. No fabricated paragraphs or sentences were found. The additional table caption and summary are allowed AI-generated accessibility features.
The URL provided in the output 'https://it.nc.gov/gicc' exactly matches the URL in the source document. No other contact information (phone numbers, emails) is present.
All sections from the one-page source document (header, agenda table, meeting dates, website link) are present in the output.
The source table has 6 data rows (excluding the header). The output table correctly reflects these 6 rows, including the nested lists in item 5.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The content of each section is fully preserved. The list of committee members and the specific meeting dates are accurately captured.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Textual content is faithful to the source. Punctuation, dates, and names match correctly. Formatting like bold and italic text for the agenda items is preserved where appropriate.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the entire JSON output and compared it against the source document. Each text segment from the original agenda, including headers, agenda items, committee names, and meeting dates, appears exactly once in the output document. There are no instances of duplicated paragraphs or repeated table rows.

The heading hierarchy in the JSON follows a logical progression starting from H2 for the main organization title ('North Carolina' and 'GEOGRAPHIC INFORMATION COORDINATING COUNCIL') and H3 for the document type ('AGENDA'). While an H1 is omitted in favor of H2, the progression (H2 to H3) is continuous and avoids skipping levels. This is considered acceptable normalization.

The main agenda table is correctly captured as a table element. It maintains the 3-column structure (TIME, AGENDA ITEM, GICC MEMBER or GUEST) found in the source. Header rows are properly identified, and the alignment of agenda items with their corresponding speakers and times is preserved across all rows, including the complex row for Item 5.

The reading order in the JSON follows the top-to-bottom, left-to-right flow of the source document: starting with the logo, then the titles and meeting logistics, followed by the agenda table, then the future meeting dates, and finally the website link. This faithfully represents the intended flow of information.

The bulleted list under agenda item 5 ('Committee Reports') is accurately captured within the table cell. Although it is rendered as text with bullet characters rather than a separate nested list structure, the items are correctly segmented and their relationship to the 'Committee Reports' heading is maintained. All items from the source are present and correctly ordered.
The government website link (https://it.nc.gov/gicc) is present and accurately transcribed at the end of the document. No unauthorized links were added, and the URL matches the source text exactly.

checks.a_analysis: The overall visual organization of the document is faithfully maintained. The title, logo, meeting details, agenda table, and future dates are all presented in the same relative order and positions as the source PDF. The transformation from a fixed PDF layout to a responsive HTML layout with a sidebar table of contents is an expected structural change for accessibility.

While most of the table is correctly rendered, row 5 (Committee Reports) is visually misrendered in terms of information mapping. In the source PDF, the sub-items (individual committees) are clearly aligned horizontally with their respective presenters. In the HTML output, the list of committees is collapsed into one cell and the list of names into another cell as continuous strings of text, breaking the visual and semantic association between committees and their reporters.
checks.b_evidence_source: Item 5 'Committee Reports' shows six bulleted committee names (e.g., 'Statewide Mapping Advisory') horizontally aligned with six specific individuals (e.g., 'Paul Badr').
checks.b_evidence_output: The 'AGENDA ITEM' cell for row 5 contains a single block of bulleted text for all committees, and the 'GICC MEMBER or GUEST' cell contains a continuous string of names ('Paul Badr Debbie Brannan Scott Lokken Dean Grantham John Farley Alex Rankin') with no visual alignment between them.

checks.a_analysis: The primary meaningful image from the source, the NCGICC logo, is correctly present and rendered in the header of the HTML document.
There is no genuine body content duplication. Section headings appearing in both the sidebar table of contents and the main body are an expected feature of accessible navigation design, not a defect.
